assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Repository Registry' section with a table listing both 'trustify-backend' (serena_backend) and 'trustify-ui' (serena_ui) as entries
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 3-8: '## Repository Registry' heading followed by a table with rows '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' and '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |'. Both repositories and their Serena instances are present.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Jira Configuration' section with all 5 fields: Project key (TC), Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID (10142), Git Pull Request custom field (customfield_10875), GitHub Issue custom field (customfield_10747)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 10-16: '## Jira Configuration' section lists all 5 fields: 'Project key: TC', 'Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432', 'Feature issue type ID: 10142', 'Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875', 'GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Code Intelligence' section that documents the mcp__<instance>__<tool> naming convention
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 18-20: '## Code Intelligence' heading followed by 'Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.' The naming convention is explicitly documented with a concrete example on lines 25-29.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '### Limitations' subheading under Code Intelligence
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 31: '### Limitations' subheading appears after the '## Code Intelligence' section (line 18), making it a subsection of Code Intelligence. It lists limitations for both serena_backend and serena_ui on lines 33-34.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md mentions discovering 2 Serena instances (serena_backend and serena_ui) from the MCP tool listing
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 5: '2 Serena instances discovered from MCP tool listing:' followed by lines 7-8 listing 'serena_backend -- identified via `mcp__serena_backend__*` tools' and 'serena_ui -- identified via `mcp__serena_ui__*` tools'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates all sections were newly added (no preserved entries since this is a greenfield setup)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md line 5: 'All sections were newly added (greenfield setup -- no existing Project Configuration section in CLAUDE.md).' The log then details each section under '## Sections Added' (lines 7-28), confirming everything was created fresh.

assertion_results.text: The Repository Registry table contains columns: Repository, Role, Serena Instance, Path -- matching project-config.template.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 5: '| Repository | Role | Serena Instance | Path |' -- the table header contains exactly the four required columns in the correct order.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers to enable security triage for the project (Step 8 opt-in prompt) and respects the user's decline -- outputs/claude-md-result.md does NOT contain a '## Security Configuration' section
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md does not contain any '## Security Configuration' section (the file ends at line 35 with the Limitations content). discovery-log.md line 33 confirms: 'User declined security triage configuration when prompted at Step 8. No Security Configuration section was added.' changes-log.md lines 32-34 also confirm: 'Security Configuration - Not added -- user declined opt-in when asked whether to enable security triage for this project'.
